mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index indicates expanding manufacturing and utility output, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer income expectations and triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and a simultaneous deceleration in industrial production creates a restrictive monetary policy hitting a slowing economy, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the simple univariate FEDFUNDS signal.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when the unemployment rate is already low, an accelerating consumer price index forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy more aggressively than priced, compressing equity multiples and generating negative returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, positive CPIAUCSL shocks are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or the strategy Sharpe ratio in this regime is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Advancing retail sales, captured by rising RSAFS, reflect resilient consumer spending that supports top-line revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, predicting positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum do not exceed the unconditional baseline with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05) over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment, a leading component of GDP that drags down broad economic activity and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,200, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payrolls and acceleration in consumer prices captures a stagflationary wage-price dynamic that erodes profit margins and forecasts negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce a directional accuracy above 55% or a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A robust monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 300 thousand signals accelerating labor demand and income growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when payroll growth exceeds 300 thousand, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
